text: The surgeon isolates 65 centimeters of small bowel for the intracorporeal neobladder, marking segments with undyed and dyed vicryl sutures. The most mobile loop of ileum is identified for urethroileal anastomosis. Eleven centimeters of bowel are isolated and marked as the zero cm point, used to measure remaining portions for the neobladder. The 44-centimeter segment is marked for pouch formation, and the discard segment is divided using a 60mm laparoscopic stapler.

The surgeon performs a side-to-side anastomosis of the ileum using a 60mm laparoscopic stapler, aligning the intestinal loops marked with dyed sutures. The bowel is then detubularized, and a 24 French chest tube is inserted into the isolated intestine. A bias incision is made toward the medial mesenteric edge using electrocautery. Interrupted sutures are placed to maintain symmetry of the posterior plate, with barbed sutures used to ensure a watertight seal. The posterior plate is rotated 90 degrees counterclockwise to align the urethro-ileal anastomosis point with the urethra.

The surgeon performs the neobladder urethral anastomosis by suturing the ileal edge to the posterior six o'clock position of the urethral stump, continuing anteriorly over a 22 French Cavalier urethral catheter to the twelve o'clock position. Interrupted sutures are used to maintain symmetry, and the lateral edges of the posterior plate are folded anteriorly and closed with a running suture to form a spherical pouch configuration.

The surgeon performs the ureteral chimney anastomosis by bringing the left ureter to the right side under the sigmoid colon. The distal ends of the ureters are resected and tailored for the anastomosis location at the chimney, with the ureteral ends spatulated. A small enterotomy is created in the afferent limb, and a brick-like anastomosis is performed using running sutures with a 4-0 Vicryl and RB-1 needle over a 7 French double J stent. The stent is inserted through a temporary 2mm mini port in the right inferior quadrant. The proximal end of the chimney is oversewn with a 3-0 Vicryl running suture to prevent stone formation. The neobladder is filled with 180ml of saline to confirm watertight closure, and ICG may be used to assess vascularity.
